summary: Cloud metadata SSRF and exfiltration to attacker-controlled out-of-band host on postinstall
details: zer0one-dnslog@1.0.3 advertises itself as a 'simple date formatting utility' but ships no real functionality (index.js only exports a `hello` stub). The package.json `postinstall` hook runs `node postinstall.js`, whose payload uses `curl` to probe multiple cloud instance-metadata endpoints — Alibaba (100.100.100.200), AWS (169.254.169.254), Tencent (metadata.tencentyun.com, 169.254.0.23), and an internal Meituan SSRF test endpoint — saves the responses to /tmp, lists `/data/`, then POSTs the concatenated output to a Burp Collaborator subdomain (h4mx6b7krgzarfehbutwabxbu20tojc8.oastify.com/metadata). On a cloud build host, instance metadata typically contains IAM role credentials; this is dependency-confusion / SSRF-probe exfiltration that fires automatically on `npm install`. The misleading description is a deceptive cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
